hero.now_serving: Now serving · Fresno · Tulare · Kern · Kings · Madera
hero.headline: Find seasonal farm work in the Central Valley.
hero.subhead: AgConn connects Central Valley farmworkers to verified seasonal jobs and CDFA-funded training programs — bilingual from day one, with a portable record of your skills that travels with you to the next harvest.

trust.line: Trusted by 2,400+ workers and 180+ verified employers across the Valley

audience.headline: A platform that knows who's on the other side.
audience.intro: Workers, employers, and training organizations each get a purpose-built experience — connected by a single record of work, in two languages, kept on file for as long as it matters.

workers.headline: Find work this week. Build a record that travels.
workers.bullet1: Real seasonal jobs, verified employers, wages on record
workers.bullet2: Free CDFA & F3 training programs with bilingual certs
workers.bullet3: SMS alerts in your language. No app store, no email needed.
workers.bullet4: A skills wallet that travels with you, harvest to harvest

employers.headline: Hire a verified crew. Skip the paperwork.
employers.bullet1: Bilingual job postings, ready in minutes — no rewrites
employers.bullet2: Applicant kanban: applied → reviewed → hired, with wage capture
employers.bullet3: DLSE/FLC license verification — admin-confirmed in 1 day
employers.bullet4: Worker search across a vetted pool by skill, county, availability

training.headline: Issue credentials. Report on outcomes.
training.bullet1: Bilingual program listings, capacity, and waitlists
training.bullet3: WIOA-aligned exports for CDFA, F3, CalJOBS reporting
training.bullet4: SMS reminders 48h + 2h before each session, bilingual

how.headline: From "hello" to your first verified hire.

1.title: Sign up with your phone
1.body: Enter your phone number, get an SMS code, you're in. No password, no app store, no email — just a one-time code in the language you prefer.

2.title: Upload a resume — or skip it
2.body: If you have one in any language, upload it once. We'll parse it and pre-fill your profile. Don't have one? Add your skills directly. Either way, you'll never re-upload again.

3.title: See real jobs near you
3.body: Filter by county, skill, or wage. Every employer carries a verified-license badge. Every wage is shown before you click. SMS alert opt-in for any saved search.

4.title: Get hired. On the record.
4.body: Apply with one tap. Track status by SMS. When you're hired, the wage is recorded — and the credential goes into your skills wallet, ready for the next harvest.

employer_showcase.headline: A dashboard built for the way you actually hire.
employer_showcase.body: Post a bilingual job in three minutes. Watch applicants land in a kanban that captures wage on hire. Move workers across the season without re-keying anything — and let your grant officer pull a clean WIOA-aligned report on Friday afternoon.

bullet1.title: Bilingual postings, automatic.
bullet1.body: Write once in either language; we generate the bilingual variant. Edit either side any time.

bullet2.title: Hire-to-record kanban.
bullet2.body: Drag from Applied → Reviewed → Hired. Each hire captures wage and creates a recordable placement.

bullet3.title: FLC verified, before posting.
bullet3.body: Submit your CA DIR/DLSE license at signup; admin confirms within 1 business day. The "Verified FLC" badge is the only signal we ask workers to trust.

verification.headline: Every employer here has been verified — by name.
verification.body: Farm Labor Contractors must hold an active CA DIR/DLSE license and a federal MSPA registration. Growers are confirmed against the California Secretary of State business registry. We don't take applicants on their word — we check the record, then re-check it nightly.
verification.bullet1: FLC: CA Labor Code §1682 license, verified manually within 1 day, then nightly via scraper
verification.bullet2: Grower: business name + EIN against CA Secretary of State entity search
verification.bullet3: License lapses or expirations are flagged automatically — workers and grant officers are notified

impact.headline: Numbers worth reporting on.
impact.body: Every metric here is grant-defensible: counted from records, exportable in WIOA-aligned formats, and audited against the source data nightly.

workers.label: Workers placed
workers.body: Verified seasonal hires across Fresno, Tulare, Kern, Kings & Madera

wage.label: Avg. wage at hire
wage.body: $3.40 above CA agricultural minimum — every hourly rate captured at hire

retention.label: Q2→Q4 retention
retention.body: Of placed workers still employed at the second-quarter follow-up

certs.label: Bilingual certificates
certs.body: Issued through CDFA & F3-funded training programs in 2026

cta.dashboard: Want to dig in? Our public dashboard updates nightly.

featured_training.headline: Skills you keep, in writing.
featured_training.body: Programs run by Fresno EOC, CRLA, and other regional partners — funded by CDFA, F3, and CalOSBA. Every completion produces a bilingual, e-signed certificate that lives in your skills wallet.

1.title: Pesticide Handler Safety Training
1.body: Learn how to handle, mix, and apply restricted-use pesticides safely. CDFA-required for licensed handlers.

2.title: OSHA Forklift Operator Certification
2.body: 3-day OSHA-aligned course. Hands-on instruction at Fresno EOC's Visalia training yard. Test included.

3.title: Small Tractor Maintenance & Repair
3.body: One-day course at Bakersfield College's ag center. Engine basics, hydraulics, troubleshooting.

4.title: Drip Irrigation & Water Stewardship
4.body: 2-day program on drip system installation, monitoring, and CA water-rights basics. Worker-focused.

1.quote: Antes pasaba dos semanas buscando trabajo cada cosecha. Ahora recibo un mensaje, aplico, y el lunes ya estoy en el campo. Y mi certificado de pesticidas vale en cualquier rancho.
1.name: María Hernández

2.quote: The kanban replaced our paper applicant binder. Wage capture happens at hire, not weeks later. When my F3 grant officer asked for a Q1 report, I exported it in two minutes — same fields she submits to CalJOBS.
2.name: Tomás Reyes

3.quote: We were issuing certificates by hand and re-keying outcomes into CalJOBS by week's end. AgConn generates the bilingual PDF, e-signs it, and the data flows straight into our CDFA quarterly. Hours saved, every cohort.
3.name: Janelle Park, MPA

testimonials.headline: From the people who use it every season.

pricing.headline: Free for workers. Always.
pricing.body: Workers and training organizations never pay. Employers start free with up to two postings — upgrade only when you need more. Annual saves two months.

free.name: Free
free.intro: For small growers and seasonal hires. Onboard the long tail of the Valley.

pro.name: Pro
pro.intro: For working FLCs and growers running multiple postings per season.
pro.feature2: Worker search across the Valley pool
pro.feature3: Priority listing on the worker app

enterprise.name: Enterprise
enterprise.intro: For workforce boards and multi-county operators with grant reporting needs.

pricing.footnote: All plans include FLC verification, bilingual postings, SMS notifications, and grant-aligned exports.

faq.headline: Questions, in plain language.
faq.intro: Real questions from real conversations with workers, FLCs, and grant officers across the Valley. Don't see yours? Ask us directly.

grant_card.headline: Need a 30-minute walkthrough of WIOA exports?
grant_card.body: Email grants@agconn.com — we'll book it within 48h.

1.q: Is AgConn really free for workers?
1.a: Yes. Workers never pay — not for signup, not for applications, not for certificates, not for SMS notifications. The platform is funded by employer subscriptions and grant partnerships.

2.q: How do you verify Farm Labor Contractors?
2.a: Every FLC must hold an active CA DIR/DLSE license and a federal MSPA registration. We confirm both manually at signup (within 1 business day) and re-check nightly via scraper. License lapses or revocations are flagged automatically and the employer's postings are paused until resolved.

3.a: Yes. Certificates are React-PDF documents e-signed under California UETA, with cryptographic verification embedded in the file. They include the issuing organization's identifier, the program funder, dates, and a unique credential ID that's verifiable from the public certificate page.

4.q: Do I need a computer to use AgConn?
4.a: No. AgConn works on any phone that can receive SMS — no computer, no smartphone, no app store, no email. Sign up with your phone number, receive job alerts by text, and apply with one tap.

5.q: What WIOA fields do AgConn exports include?
5.a: Exports include all required WIOA Title I fields plus CalJOBS-specific extensions: PIRL elements 100–1900, demographic data (where consented), placement details with employer + wage + start date, training program completions, credential IDs, and follow-up retention. Available as CSV and XLSX, with a custom field mapper for state-specific overlays.

6.q: Can I export my data if I leave the platform?
6.a: Yes — at any time, in JSON or CSV, with no fee and no exit ramp. Workers can export their full profile + applications + certificates. Employers can export their postings + applicant pipeline + hires. Training orgs can export rosters + completions + certificate metadata.

7.q: When will AgConn expand beyond the Central Valley?
7.a: Imperial County and the Salinas Valley are next on the roadmap (target Q4 2026), pending grant alignment with regional CDFA partners. Outside California will require state-by-state legal and licensing diligence — we're starting that work but won't commit a date until verification infrastructure is reliable.

8.a: Yes. Worker personal data is never shared without explicit consent. Employers see only the profiles of applicants who apply to them. Grant reports are aggregated and anonymous. We comply with CCPA, FERPA (for training data), and federal WIOA privacy policy.

final.headline: Start today.
final.body: No credit card. No app store. No long form. Workers sign up with a phone in under a minute. Employers post their first job free. Training organizations apply for an org account in a click.

waitlist.already_confirmed: You're already on the list. We'll keep you posted.

confirmed.headline: You're in.
confirmed.body: Thanks for confirming. We saved your spot on the AgConn list — we'll reach out the moment we open in your area, in the language you signed up with.

already.headline: You're already in.
already.body: This email is already on the AgConn list — no need to confirm again. We'll be in touch when we open in your area.

expired.headline: That link's a little old.
expired.body: Confirmation links expire after 7 days. Sign up again from the homepage and we'll send a fresh link.

invalid.headline: We couldn't read that link.
invalid.body: The confirmation link looks incomplete or has already been used. Try signing up again from the homepage.

unsubscribed.headline: You're off the list.
unsubscribed.body: We removed you from the AgConn list — no more emails from us. If this was a mistake, you can sign up again from the homepage at any time.

already.headline: You're already off the list.
already.body: This email isn't on the AgConn list — nothing more to do. If you'd like to opt back in, sign up again from the homepage.

invalid.headline: We couldn't read that link.
invalid.body: The unsubscribe link looks incomplete. If you're still receiving emails you don't want, reply to one and we'll remove you manually.
